name: Apono Access Management API
what_it_does: Developer-focused just-in-time (JIT) access management platform with REST API — enabling agents to request, approve, and revoke time-limited access to databases, cloud resources, and Kubernetes clusters without permanent privilege grants.
use_cases: Requesting just-in-time database access for incident response via API
Automating access approval workflows based on on-call schedules and alerts
Querying access grant history and audit logs for compliance reporting agents
Revoking elevated access automatically after time-limited session expires
Integrating JIT access requests into ChatOps and Slack-based workflows
not_for: Permanent access management — Apono is specifically for temporary JIT access
Consumer-facing identity — enterprise and developer access only
Organizations without infrastructure resources needing privileged access
best_when: An agent manages on-demand privileged access to infrastructure, automating JIT access grants and revocations without permanent credential exposure.
avoid_when: You need permanent access management or traditional RBAC — Apono is for ephemeral access patterns.

auth.notes: OAuth2 for user-facing access requests. API key for service accounts and automation. Scopes for request, approve, revoke, and audit operations. Machine identity support for automated workflows.

pricing.notes: Enterprise-focused SaaS. Pricing based on resources managed and users. Cloud-native company with strong developer access focus. Competes with Teleport and HashiCorp Boundary.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per API key; documented in API reference

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: OpenAPI reference documentation. REST API covers access requests, approvals, and audit. Webhook delivery for access state changes. Error responses are JSON with descriptive messages.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Access request creation is not idempotent — creates new request each call. Access grant status queries are idempotent. Revocation is idempotent (revoking already-revoked access is safe).
known_agent_gotchas: Approval workflows may require human approver action — build approval wait logic
Access grants expire automatically — agents must handle expiry and re-request if needed
Resource connector setup required before JIT access to specific resources
Time-zone handling for expiry times — use UTC consistently

security_score_components.security_notes: SOC2 compliant. Zero permanent privilege philosophy. TLS enforced. OAuth2 for user access requests. Audit logging of all access grants and revocations. JIT model reduces standing privilege exposure.
